zh.lifeSummary: Hamo de Crevequer de Crèvecœur Baron of Chatham Baron of Chatham是Robert de Crevequer的兒子，於1210年出生在英國肯特利德斯。他於大約1219年與Maud d'Avranches結婚。他們至少是4個女兒的父母。他去世於1263年4月6日在英國肯特享年53歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Hamo de Crevequer de Crèvecœur Baron of Chatham Baron of Chatham是Robert de Crevequer的儿子，于1210年出生在英国肯特利德斯。他于大约1219年与Maud d'Avranches结婚。他们至少是4个女儿的父母。他去世于1263年4月6日在英国肯特享年53岁。

nameMeanings.name: Baron
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French, English, Dutch, Polish, Czech, Romanian, and Croatian; Breton (also Le Baron): from Old French barun, baron, Middle English barun, baroun, Middle Dutch baroen, Polish, Czech, Romanian, and dialectal Croatian baron ‘baron’ (i.e. a nobleman ranking below an earl and above a knight), a title of nobility which was in England also awarded to certain freemen of the cities of London and York and of the Cinque Ports. As a surname it is unlikely to be a status name denoting a person of rank. Generally, it referred to service in a baronial household or was acquired as a nickname by a peasant who had ideas above his station or applied to a man showing characteristics of a nobleman. See also 3 and 4 below, compare Barron and Lebaron .
French: habitational name from (Le) Baron, the name of several places in various parts of France. Compare   Lebaron  .
German: status name for a freeman or baron, from barūn ‘imperial or church official’, a loanword in Middle High German from Old French (see 1 above).

variants.name: Barron
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Jesus, Manuel, Francisco, Carlos, Miguel, Raul, Pedro, Ruben, Alfredo, Luis.

Scottish: status name from Scots baron, which in the Highlands denoted a small proprietary landholder. In Angus it alluded specifically to a tenant of one of the small baronies attached to the Abbey of Coupar Angus. The surname was taken by Scottish migrants to Ulster. Compare Baron .
Scottish: shortened form of McBarron, from Scottish Gaelic Mac a' Bharain ‘son of the baron’. See Baron and 1 above.

variants.name: Boron
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Krystyna, Edyta, Leszek, Mieczyslaw, Mieczyslawa, Wladyslaw.
Polish (Boroń): from a pet form of the personal names Borzysław or Borzymir, formed with the element bor ‘to fight’ (from Old Slavic boriti se).
French: habitational name from a place so called in Franche-Comté.

variants.name: Baro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Mario, Manuel, Ana, Angel, Carlos, Cesar, Fernando, Jesus, Jose, Orlando, Ramon, Rosalia.  Italian Guido, Angelo, Carmelo, Federico, Lucio.
Catalan (Baró): status name from baró ‘free man, baron’ (see Baron 1), or a habitational name from a place so named.
Hungarian (Báró): status name from báró ‘baron’. In the form Baró, it could be from a pet form of the personal names Barabás (see Barabas ) or Bertalan.

variants.name: Barona
meanings: Spanish: from the feminine form of Barón ‘baron’ (see Baron ), applied as a nickname or as an occupational name for a member of the household of a baroness.
Italian: probably from the feminine form of   Barone  . The name is concentrated in Sondrio province (Lombardy).

variants.name: Barren
meanings: English: probably a variant of   Baron   or   Barran  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Korean Kyong, Kyong Suk, Young, Chae, Chi Young, Hang, Kil, Rin, Sang Hun, Chang, Chong, Insoo, Kum, Sun Young, Yom, Young Hwan.  Cambodian Kiwan, Oeurn, Phoeun, Pich, Saroeuth, Seung, Sophath, Sophea, Yoeun, Phan, Son.  Indian Shanti, Harendra.
Indian (Uttar Pradesh): Brahmin name, from Sanskrit om, the Hindu sacred and mystic syllable uttered at the beginning of prayers.
Cambodian: written ឱម, corresponding to the exclamation of solemn affirmation, of Sanskrit origin (see 1 above), chanted by Buddhist monks.

variants.name: Oum
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Heang, Hye, Kam, Kan, Kheng, Khin, Kun, Sang, Sok, Phong, Yom, Yoo, Youn, Cuong, Phal, Samnang, Tin.  Cambodian Sokha, Chheang, Choeun, Moeun, Roeun, Sambath, Samoeun, Savuth, Soeun, Sopheap.
Cambodian: written អ៊ុំ, from a Khmer word denoting an elder sibling of one's parent (i.e. ‘uncle’ or ‘aunt’). Compare   Om   3 and   Um   2.

nameMeanings.name: Chatham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (West Midlands and Lancashire): variant of   Cheetham  .
English: habitational name from Chatham in Kent or possibly from Chatham Green in Essex, both named from Celtic cēd ‘wood’ (modern Welsh coed) + Old English hām ‘homestead’.

variants.name: Cheetham
meanings: English: variant of   Cheatham  .

nameMeanings.name: Hamo
meanings: This is still found as a male baptismal name. It is derived from Hamo, who came over with the Conqueror; perhaps from the Icelandic hamr, a skin found in ulf-hamr, the nickname of a mythological king, which Cleasby translates 'wolf-skin.'

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: ‘Bright’.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: ‘Bright’.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: Hamo de Crevequer de Crèvecœur Baron of Chatham Baron of Chatham was born in 1210, in Leeds, Kent, England as the son of Robert de Crevequer. He married Maud d'Avranches about 1219. They were the parents of at least 4 daughters. He died on 6 April 1263, in Kent, England, at the age of 53.

fr.lifeSummary: Hamo de Crevequer de Crèvecœur Baron of Chatham Baron of Chatham est né en 1210, à Leeds, Kent, Angleterre, fils de Robert de Crevequer. Il a épousé Maud d'Avranches environ 1219. Ils ont eu au moins 4 filles. Il est décédé le 6 avril 1263, à Kent, Angleterre, à l’âge de 53 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Hamo de Crevequer de Crèvecœur Baron of Chatham Baron of Chatham wurde 1210 in Leeds, Kent, England, als Sohn von Robert de Crevequer geboren. Er hat um 1219 Maud d'Avranches geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Töchter. Er starb am 6. April 1263 im Alter von 53 Jahren in Kent, England.

it.lifeSummary: Hamo de Crevequer de Crèvecœur Baron of Chatham Baron of Chatham è nato il nel 1210 nel Leeds, Kent, England come figlio di Robert de Crevequer. Lui sposato Maud d'Avranches circa 1219. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figlie. Morì il 6 aprile 1263, nel Kent, England, all'età di 53 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Hamo de Crevequer de Crèvecœur Baron of Chatham Baron of Chathamは1210年にイギリスケントリーズでRobert de Crevequerの息子として生まれた。約1219年にMaud d'Avranchesと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の娘の親であった。彼は1263年4月6日にイギリスケントで53歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Hamo de Crevequer de Crèvecœur Baron of Chatham Baron of Chatham(은)는 1210년 영국 켄트 리드즈에서 Robert de Crevequer의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 대략 1219년 Maud d'Avranches(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1263년 4월 6일 영국 켄트에서 53세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Hamo de Crevequer de Crèvecœur Baron of Chatham Baron of Chatham nasceu em 1210, em Leeds, Kent, Inglaterra filho de Robert de Crevequer. Ele casou-se com Maud d'Avranches aproximadamente 1219. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em 6 de abril de 1263, em Kent, Inglaterra, com 53 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Hamo de Crevequer de Crèvecœur Baron of Chatham Baron of Chatham родился в 1210 г. в местности лидс, кэнт, Англия. Его отца звали Robert de Crevequer. Он женился на женщине по имени Maud d'Avranches около 1219 г. У них было по крайней мере 4 дочери. Он умер 6 апреля 1263 г. в местности кэнт, Англия в возрасте 53 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Hamo de Crevequer de Crèvecœur Baron of Chatham Baron of Chatham nació en 1210, en Leeds, Kent, Inglaterra como hijo de Robert de Crevequer. Se casó con Maud d'Avranches aproximadamente 1219. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijas. Murió el 6 de abril de 1263, en Kent, Inglaterra, a la edad de 53 años.
